summary: The PoC demonstrates zip bomb DoS vulnerability through code analysis and attack vector demonstration. The decompression endpoint lacks size limits, compression ratio checks, and disk space validation.
expected_security_impact: Zip bomb attack can exhaust disk space exponentially. A small compressed archive (42KB) decompresses to gigabytes, filling storage and causing denial of service for the entire Wings node and all hosted servers.
actual_observed_result: Code analysis confirmed the vulnerability: FileController.php:193-208 decompress endpoint only uses set_time_limit(300) without resource limits, DecompressFilesRequest has no size validation, and DaemonFileRepository passes archives directly to Wings without bounds checking. No compression ratio validation or disk space checks exist.
verification_method: Static code analysis verifying: (1) missing decompression size limits in FileController, (2) no compression ratio validation, (3) insufficient request validation, (4) absence of disk space checks. Attack vector demonstration shows classic zip bomb pattern.
evidence_produced_by_run: FileController.php:193-208 confirmed to decompress archives with only set_time_limit(300)
No maximum decompression size limit found
No compression ratio validation (classic zip bomb protection)
DecompressFilesRequest.php has no file size or compression rules
DaemonFileRepository.php:227-247 passes archive path without bounds checking
limitations_or_blockers: Full runtime reproduction requires: Pterodactyl Panel, Wings daemon, valid authentication, server instance
Actual zip bomb creation skipped to avoid resource exhaustion in PoC environment
Docker PoC demonstrates vulnerability through code analysis

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones pterodactyl/panel, checks out 1.0-develop, mounts at /target-repo
